title: Marble Brownies
ingredients: ¾ cup Ghiradelli Double Double Chocolate Chips or Hersheys Dark Chocolate chips
instructions: Preheat oven to 325 degrees.
Lightly butter a 4 quart Pyrex oblong pan (15x10 1/2x 2 inches).
Melt chocolate and butter in top of double boiler or in microwave, stirring every 30 seconds if using microwave to prevent burning.
Cool melted chocolate-butter mixture for 5–10 minutes.
Put sugar in large mixing bowl.
Add chocolate-butter mixture and beat until well combined.
Add eggs one at a time, blending well after each egg.
Add vanilla.
Scrape bowl and blend for 30 seconds.
Add flour and fold in by hand.
Set brownie mixture aside.
Place all cheesecake batter ingredients (cream cheese, flour, sugar, egg, vanilla) in medium mixing bowl.
Beat cheesecake batter until well combined.
Spread 2/3 of brownie batter in prepared pan.
Drop cheesecake mixture by spoonful in mounds to make about 5 rows of four mounds across.
Drop remaining brownie batter next to cream cheese mounds, but not on top of them.
Run a chopstick or end of a skinny spoon back and forth the length of the pan, making parallel lines about 1 ½ inches apart.
Make similar lines in the other direction of the pan.
Shake the pan gently back and forth to level the batter.
Sprinkle chocolate chips over top of batter.
Bake for 45–50 minutes.
Cool brownies completely.
Melt the chocolate for frosting in double boiler or carefully in microwave, ensuring chocolate does not burn.
Cool chocolate slightly.
Pour evaporated milk and sugar into electric blender.
Pulse for 3 seconds.
Add melted chocolate.
Blend frosting on medium-low speed until thickened, about 1 minute.
Using a frosting spatula, spread frosting over the surface of the brownies.
Allow frosting to sit for 1 hour before cutting.
notes: When melting chocolate in microwave, stir every 30 seconds to prevent burning
Cream cheese for cheesecake batter should be at room temperature
Ghiradelli Double Double Chocolate Chips or Hersheys Dark Chocolate chips can be used
Allow 1 hour for frosting to set before cutting
